plan_adherence_notes: All 7 plan steps were executed faithfully. The test file contains exactly the describe blocks, test cases, and assertions specified in the plan. 13 tests across 5 components, all passing. Minor: the plan said 'danger-colored styling' for ErrorMessage — the test checks `el.style.color === 'var(--color-danger)'` which is correct but the plan could have been more specific about what 'danger-colored styling' means (color vs border vs background). Not a real gap since the implementation correctly tests the most relevant style property.
missed_questions: No question about how to verify ErrorMessage's visual styling (which CSS properties to assert on) — though this was straightforward from reading the component source
No question about whether the test file should also cover accessibility (aria attributes, roles) — not present in the components, so not a real gap, but worth asking to confirm scope
reason: Execution closely followed the plan. All 7 steps completed, 13 tests pass, and the full suite (113 tests) remains green. Research was thorough: 5 well-scoped questions with high-confidence answers, all 6 assumptions verified correct. The test file correctly uses real utility implementations (no mocks), plain render (no unnecessary providers), and tests observable behavior rather than implementation details. Test assertions match the actual component source code: formatProbability output format, statusColor CSS variable values, Spinner SVG attributes, ErrorMessage danger color, and StatusBadge textTransform. The single-file grouping decision was well-justified given the small, related nature of the UI components.
